description: Expand micro509's WebCrypto-aligned PKI algorithm surface with RSA-PSS, ECDSA P-521, and low-cost key-container interop.

tasks.title: Add algorithm OIDs and public type expansions
tasks.description:
Add the OIDs, unions, and public input/result shapes needed for RSA-PSS and ECDSA P-521. Done when:
- `src/oids.ts` contains `rsassaPss`, `mgf1`, `ecdsaWithSHA512`, and `secp521r1`
- RSA key inputs gain `scheme?: 'pkcs1-v1_5' | 'pss'` without breaking current callers
- ECDSA curve unions include `P-521` anywhere current public APIs expose `P-256`/`P-384`
- verify error unions include a typed `unsupported_signature_algorithm_parameters` path

tasks.title: Preserve raw AlgorithmIdentifier params
tasks.description:
Preserve raw signature and SPKI algorithm parameters in parsed cert and CSR outputs. Done when:
- `src/parse.ts` exposes raw params DER for signature and public-key algorithm identifiers when present
- existing OID fields stay stable for backward compatibility
- malformed ASN.1 still fails at parse time instead of degrading silently

tasks.title: Add constrained RSA-PSS parameter model
tasks.description:
Implement the internal RSA-PSS parameter parse/encode helpers shared by verify and builders. Done when:
- one internal model represents hash, MGF1 hash, saltLength, and trailerField
- parser accepts only the shipped SHA-256/384/512 plus MGF1 subset
- encoder emits explicit parameters for builder-generated RSA-PSS signatures
- unsupported-but-well-formed params are distinguishable from malformed DER

tasks.title: Wire RSA-PSS into certificate and CSR verification
tasks.description:
Teach verification flows to accept supported RSA-PSS artifacts. Done when:
- cert chain verification accepts RSA-PSS-signed chains for the shipped parameter profile
- CSR verification accepts RSA-PSS-signed requests for the same profile
- RSA key import for verification chooses `RSA-PSS` vs `RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5` by intended scheme
- unsupported params fail with typed verification errors, not generic crashes

tasks.title: Wire RSA-PSS into cert and CSR builders
tasks.description:
Add explicit RSA-PSS signing support to certificate and CSR creation APIs. Done when:
- `createCertificate()` and `createCertificateSigningRequest()` accept an explicit signature profile input
- omitted signature profile keeps current behavior for existing callers
- RSA-PSS signing defaults `saltLength` to digest size when omitted
- emitted AlgorithmIdentifier values carry explicit RSA-PSS parameters

tasks.title: Add ECDSA P-521 end-to-end support
tasks.description:
Make P-521 a first-class ECDSA option across keys, signing, and verification. Done when:
- key generation and key import accept `P-521`
- signing maps `P-521` to `ecdsaWithSHA512` with correct raw-signature width handling
- verification accepts `ecdsaWithSHA512` on `secp521r1` keys
- cert and CSR create/verify flows work with P-521 artifacts

tasks.title: Widen encrypted private-key interop
tasks.description:
Expand low-cost encrypted-key compatibility using existing WebCrypto primitives. Done when:
- PBES2 supports AES-128-CBC, AES-192-CBC, and AES-256-CBC
- PBKDF2 PRFs include HMAC-SHA1 and HMAC-SHA256 where the key APIs can absorb them
- traditional PEM AES-128/192/256-CBC variants import for RSA and EC private keys
- unsupported schemes like 3DES still reject explicitly

tasks.title: Add fixtures and regression coverage
tasks.description:
Add focused coverage for the new algorithm and key-container surface. Done when:
- RSA-PSS positive and negative cert/CSR fixtures exist
- P-521 keygen/import/sign/verify coverage exists
- encrypted-key interop fixtures cover positive and negative cases
- regression tests prove current RSA PKCS#1 v1.5, P-256, P-384, and Ed25519 behavior stays intact

tasks.title: Sync docs to shipped algorithm matrix
tasks.description:
Update public docs only after the implementation and fixtures land. Done when:
- `README.md`, `docs/PKIX-SCOPE.md`, and `docs/FUTURE.md` describe the exact shipped matrix
- docs name RSA-PSS, ECDSA P-521, and key-container interop precisely
- docs do not imply DSA, Ed448, or full WebCrypto parity
- every user-facing claim maps to code and tests that now exist
